mechanism_narrative: Rising real retail sales (RSAFS adjusted for inflation) indicate robust consumer spending, driving corporate earnings expectations higher and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the rank IC of one-month changes in RSAFS against subsequent 20-day S&P 500 returns is below 0.03 over a rolling 3-year window, or if RSAFS increases are followed by negative returns in more than half of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A declining unemployment rate below 3.5% signals an overheating labor market where wage pressures compress profit margins and prompt preemptive Fed tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If 60-day forward S&P 500 returns are positive in more than 40% of instances when UNRATE is below 3.5%, or if the signal does not coincide with a rising unemployment trend.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the real interest rate (FEDFUNDS minus trailing 12-month CPI inflation) signals an accommodative monetary stance that lowers the equity risk premium, boosting stock prices within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If a decreasing real rate is associated with negative 5-day forward returns in more than 40% of cases, or if the rank IC of real rate changes is below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below 2%, rising housing starts (HOUST) indicate increased residential investment and economic momentum, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days. In a low-rate regime, the housing channel amplifies equity gains.
failure_condition: If rising HOUST during FEDFUNDS below 2% leads to negative or flat 20-day returns in over 50% of events, or if the correlation flips sign when tested out-of-sample.

mechanism_narrative: Growing industrial production (INDPRO) signals rising manufacturing output and economic strength, which supports higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If a one-month increase in INDPRO is followed by negative 60-day returns in more than 35% of instances, or if the predictive rank IC over any 5-year window is below 0.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the federal funds rate above 4% signals a restrictive monetary policy aimed at curbing inflation, increasing discount rates and reducing equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If positive 20-day returns occur in more than 45% of periods when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4%, or if the median return following such threshold crossings is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: Joint increases in payroll employment (PAYEMS) and decreases in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) indicate a strengthening labor market that boosts consumer confidence and spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the combined signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE does not produce a positive average 5-day forward return, or if the signal is not significantly different from zero (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below 2%, rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) signals demand-pull pressures and potential future tightening, but initially boosts equity returns via inflation pass-through to revenues and positive real activity expectations over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If rising CPI during low-rate periods is followed by negative 60-day returns in more than 40% of cases, or if the predictive relationship disappears in out-of-sample tests.
